↳ Chicago, IL

Chad
Weaver.

I'm a data analyst and civic tech builder based in Chicago. By day I work as a Business Insights Analyst at McDonald's, where I build ETL pipelines, Tableau dashboards, and data tools that drive decisions at global scale.

Outside of work, I'm obsessed with building tools that make people think more clearly — products that sit at the intersection of data, epistemics, and civic life. I believe good software can be a force for empathy and collective understanding.

I'm a systems thinker who sweats the details. I care about sustainability, empathy, and clarity — both in the products I build and in the way I work.

What I care about
◈
Systems thinking
I look for root causes, feedback loops, and second-order effects. Good analysis means understanding the whole system, not just the surface metric.
◎
Empathy in design
Every tool I build is for real people making real decisions. I care deeply about how products feel to use — not just whether they technically work.
◇
Epistemic clarity
I'm fascinated by how people form beliefs and how bad information spreads. I want to build tools that help people reason better — together.
Right now
Building
Civic Compass — live and in active development. Helping people understand their moral archetype and engage with politics more thoughtfully.
Working on
The Argument Evaluator — a tool for stress-testing claims with AI-powered fallacy detection. Coming soon.
Day job
Business Insights Analyst at McDonald's — delivery analytics, ETL pipelines, global reporting.
Thinking about
Tools for collective reasoning, epistemic infrastructure, and how data can make civic participation more meaningful.
Want to work together or just say hi? Let's talk.
